The correct revenue measure is clearly the line 'Revenue and other operating income', which matches typical revenue magnitudes and precedes operating expenses. candidates.why: Direct production/input costs; typical COGS line.
Primarily personnel costs, more SG&A-like for a diversified group, but could include some production labor; secondary candidate.
Appears to be a broad operating cost subtotal including multiple components, not pure COGS; would risk double counting.
Selling expense, not COGS.
Overhead, not direct cost of sales.
Miscellaneous other income/expense, not COGS.
Selling expense, not COGS.
Miscellaneous other income/expense, not COGS.
Overhead/other opex, not direct COGS.
Unusual items, not recurring COGS.
Net operating figure, not a pure COGS line.
Non-operating, not COGS.
Lease-related overhead, not primary COGS.
Balance sheet inventory, not P&L cost of sales.
Balance sheet accruals, not P&L COGS line.

candidates.why: Top-line revenue, not SG&A.
Appears to be a total operating cost bucket that likely includes COGS; using it for SG&A would mix COGS and overhead, contrary to instructions.
Other operating income, not SG&A expense.
Other operating expenses consistent with SG&A/overhead.

candidates.why: Amortization (D&A), which must be excluded from SG&A per instructions.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. No explicit SG&A subtotal exists. Following hierarchy, selected multiple overhead-type expense components (personnel, advertising, rent/leases, miscellaneous operating expense, and audit/other professional fees) while excluding COGS, totals, balancing items, and D&A/impairments. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Employee benefits expense, [Income Statement] Advertising Expense, [Income Statement] Rental Expense, [Income Statement] Variable Financial Lease Expenses, [Income Statement] Short-Term Lease Cost, [Income Statement] Audit Fees, [Income Statement] Audit-Related Fees, [Income Statement] Tax Fees, [Income Statement] Other Fees, [Income Statement] Audit Fees to Other

candidates.why: Explicit R&D expense line in the income statement.
Balance sheet inventory, not an R&D expense line.
Capitalized development costs on balance sheet, not current period R&D expense.
Accumulated amortization of development costs, not current period R&D expense.

variables.notes: Uses the explicit R&D expense line from the income statement; no need to rely on balance sheet development cost items.

candidates.why: Net interest line; good fallback if detailed components are unavailable for some years.

Portion of interest capitalized; informative but not pure expense in P&L.

Income allocation, not interest expense.

variables.notes: Provided a priority list of explicit interest expense lines, preferring the main gross expense labels, then specific components (leases, pensions), then net-of-capitalized and net interest, and finally capitalized interest-related lines as lowest-priority fallbacks.

candidates.row_label: Carried against equity in associates and joint ventures

Relates to associates, not total shareholders' equity.

candidates.row_label: Investments accounted for using equity method
candidates.why: Investment asset, not equity.
Component of equity only.
Major component of equity but excludes other reserves and NCI.

candidates.row_label: Items charged to equity in associates and JV
candidates.why: Reserve component, not total equity.
Subcomponent of equity.
Represents equity attributable to owners (since adding Non-controlling interests yields 'Equity'); best proxy for BE excluding NCI.
Appears to equal Total equity + Non-controlling interests, i.e., total equity including NCI; less suitable than 'Total equity' for BE which should exclude NCI.

variables.notes: Balance sheet shows: Total equity + Non-controlling interests = Equity. Thus 'Total equity' corresponds to equity attributable to owners (excluding NCI) and is the appropriate BE measure.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation to NCI; not the balance sheet amount but confirms presence of NCI concept.
Duplicate label for NCI in income statement context.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive income attributable to non-controlling interests
candidates.why: Comprehensive income allocation, not the balance sheet minority interest balance.
Balance sheet minority interest line; primary target for MIB.
Alternate label for same balance sheet NCI item, likely used in some periods; good backup.

variables.notes: Uses the balance sheet NCI lines as a priority list for minority interest (MIB), preferring 'Non-controlling interests' and falling back to 'Minority Interest' for other periods.
